name: CALI Research Substrate
mission: Provide CALI with a preserve-first offline research cache.
Mirror high-value public datasets for local retrieval and analysis.
Support embeddings, vector indexes, cross-reference tables, and entity graphs.
Preserve long-term Cali-centered continuity independent of fragile APIs.

storage_zones.api_cache: Cached raw and normalized API payloads.
storage_zones.datasets: Bulk mirrors and curated offline corpora.
storage_zones.embeddings: Semantic embeddings grouped by domain and source family.
storage_zones.manifests: Drive manifests, registries, and build plans.
storage_zones.snapshots: Dated point-in-time copies of important data assets.
storage_zones.vector_indexes: Vector search artifacts and ANN structures.

example_public_source_families: NASA imagery metadata and Earth observation catalogs
operating_rules: Treat R:\ as preserve-first storage, not scratch space.
Avoid deletion or major cleanup without explicit confirmation.
Prefer manifest-driven additions so future sessions inherit context.
Separate code from data unless the user explicitly wants them co-located.
Archive or snapshot before replacing important structures when practical.
strategic_value: Reduces dependence on rate-limited or unstable APIs.
Makes local plus online synthesis possible.
Improves reproducibility and continuity across long projects.
Creates a sovereign research engine instead of a thin API client.
